Let me verify the actual runtime behavior of `format.ts` edge cases to ground the test design and any defect claims.
I have enough grounding. Key empirical findings:
- `formatSignedCurrency` uses **U+2212** (typographic minus), not ASCII `-` — a test must assert the right glyph or it'll spuriously fail.
- NaN flows through unguarded → `"$NaN"` / `"NaN%"`.
- `rankings` has **no extractable pure logic** — the only delta computation (`prev - now`) is trapped inline in `KeywordTable.tsx`, and it renders rank **drops** identically to "no change" (`—`), hiding regressions. That's a latent defect.

Let me persist the ADR to the repo decision log.
ADR persisted. Emitting the deliverable and protocol messages.

{"user_message": {"title": "Architecture Analysis — Test Harness + Pure-Logic Coverage · ADR-0001 (local-seo-agency)", "content": "## ADR-0001 · Lightweight test runner + unit coverage for high-value pure logic
*Status: Accepted. Persisted to `docs/adr/ADR-0001-test-harness-and-pure-logic-coverage.md`. First ADR for **local-seo-agency** (prior ADR-0001/0002 in memory belong to a different repo, `ai-paralegal`).*

### Context
`local-seo-agency` (Next.js 16 + React 19 + TS-strict, ESM) ships **zero tests** — no `test` script, no `*.test.*` files. Inspected the two named targets:

- **`src/lib/format.ts`** — 4 genuinely pure `Intl.NumberFormat` functions. Verified at runtime: `formatSignedCurrency(-1234.5)` → `"−$1,234.50"` using a **typographic minus U+2212, not ASCII `-`** (a test asserting `"-$…"` would spuriously fail); `0` and `-0` → `"$0.00"` (no sign); rounding is half-expand (`2.005→$2.01`); **NaN is unguarded** → `"$NaN"` / `"NaN%"` leak to the UI.
- **`src/features/rankings`** — `data.ts` is **static mock data only; no pure computation exists to test.** The single delta calc is trapped inline in JSX in `KeywordTable.tsx` (`const change = k.prev - k.now`). Map-pack / share-of-voice are pure presentation — nothing to unit-test.

### Decision
1. **Runner: built-in `node --test` via `tsx`** — no Jest/Vitest. Add one devDep (`tsx`) + `"test": "node --import tsx --test \"src/**/*.test.ts\""`. Native ESM + TS-strict, fast, minimal surface.
2. **Cover `format.ts`** against real edges (negative, 0, −0, NaN, very large, rounding) — assert the **U+2212** minus explicitly.
3. **Extract `rankDelta(prev, now)`** into a pure `src/features/rankings/rank.ts`, unit-test it, refactor `KeywordTable.tsx` to consume it.
4. **Fix a real latent defect** found while reading the branch: rank **drops** (`now > prev`) currently render identically to *no change* (`—`), hiding regressions. Render down-moves distinctly (`↓ N`), driven test-first.
5. **NaN handling** — pin current behavior with tests; decide a finite-guard as a scoped follow-up rather than silently change it.

### Consequences
- **+** Green, <1s suite guards money/percent formatting + rank math; extracting `rankDelta` removes logic from JSX → testable + reusable.
- **−** One devDep (`tsx`) + small `KeywordTable.tsx` refactor.
- **Defect:** rank drops are invisible today (no drop row in mock data); fix changes rendered output for any down-move.
- **Scope:** map-pack/share-of-voice intentionally untested (no computation).
- **UNVERIFIED:** suite not yet run in CI; assumes Node ≥22 test-runner glob support.

### Task breakdown — 5 tasks · 11 story points
| # | Task | Scope | Pts |
|---|------|-------|-----|
| 1 | Wire test runner | Add `tsx` devDep + `test` script; one smoke test green | 2 |
| 2 | `format.ts` tests | All 4 fns; edges neg/0/−0/NaN/large; assert U+2212 minus + half-expand rounding | 3 |
| 3 | Extract `rankDelta()` | New `rankings/rank.ts` (up/down/flat + magnitude); refactor `KeywordTable.tsx` | 2 |
| 4 | `rankDelta` tests + fix drop defect | Test-first; render down-moves as `↓ N` not `—`; cover improve/flat/drop/large | 3 |
| 5 | NaN-guard decision | Pin `$NaN`/`NaN%`; decide+implement finite-guard or accept | 1 |

```chart
Wire runner: 2
format.ts tests: 3
Extract rankDelta: 2
rankDelta tests + fix: 3
NaN-guard: 1
```

**Verdict:** Change fits the architecture cleanly — **11 pts**. One real defect to fix (invisible rank drops). Ready for a delivery agent via `architecture.analysis.completed`.", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}

{"user_message": {"title": "Code Review — REQUEST_CHANGES · local-seo-agency (rankings + format.ts)", "content": "## Code Review — local-seo-agency
**Scope:** files flagged by the upstream architecture handoff (ADR-0001): `src/features/rankings/components/KeywordTable.tsx`, `src/lib/format.ts`, `src/features/rankings/data.ts`/`types.ts`. No new code changeset exists yet — this reviews the committed code the plan targets and validates its defect claims with file:line evidence.

> Note: upstream output referenced `ai-paralegal` from stale memory but correctly identified the live repo as **local-seo-agency**. Verified: the codebase connector resolves to `local-seo-agency`. No scope/security concern.

### Critical
_None._

### High — Correctness
- **`src/features/rankings/components/KeywordTable.tsx:25,35-42`** — Rank **drops are rendered identically to no-change.** `const change = k.prev - k.now` is positive for an improvement, **negative for a drop** (`now > prev`), and zero when flat. The JSX only branches `change > 0 ? "↑ {change}" : "—"`, so every drop collapses into `"—"` with neutral `text-muted` styling — visually indistinguishable from a stable ranking. Regressions are silently hidden. *Currently latent:* all rows in `data.ts:19-24` have `prev ≥ now`, so no live row triggers it — but any real down-move will be invisible.
  - **Fix:** Extract a pure `rankDelta(prev, now)` helper (`src/features/rankings/rank.ts`) returning direction + magnitude, then render three cases:
    ```tsx
    {change > 0 ? `↑ ${change}` : change < 0 ? `↓ ${-change}` : "—"}
    ```
    and pick the class on `change < 0 ? text-danger : change > 0 ? text-success : text-muted`. Extracting the helper also makes the logic unit-testable (it is untestable while trapped inline in JSX).

### Medium — Correctness / Robustness
- **`src/lib/format.ts:20-23, 29-31, 16-18`** — Numeric formatters have **no finite-value guard.** `formatSignedCurrency(NaN)` → sign ternary yields `""` (NaN is neither `>0` nor `<0`) and `usd.format(NaN)` → `"$NaN"`; `formatPercent(NaN)` → `"NaN%"`. A NaN from any upstream calculation leaks raw to the UI.
  - **Fix:** Guard at the top of each formatter, e.g. `if (!Number.isFinite(value)) return "—";` (decide the sentinel deliberately — pin current behavior with a test first if a silent change is risky).

### Low — Quality / Maintainability
- **`src/lib/format.ts:16-31`** — All four exported formatters (`formatCurrency`, `formatSignedCurrency`, `formatNumber`, `formatPercent`) have **no call sites anywhere in `src`** (grep-verified). They are correct but currently dead code — either wire them into the dashboard cards (which appear to hold hardcoded strings) or they ship untested and unused. Non-blocking.
- **`package.json`** — No `test` script and zero `*.test.*` files in the repo. ADR-0001's plan (`node --test` via `tsx`) is a sound, low-surface choice — endorsed. Not a code defect.
- **`src/lib/format.ts:21`** — Good: the sign uses U+2212 (`−`), the typographic minus, not ASCII `-`. Any test must assert the U+2212 glyph or it will spuriously fail. (Confirming the upstream finding.)

---
### Verdict: **REQUEST_CHANGES**
A confirmed High-severity correctness defect exists in committed code: rank drops are rendered as `"—"`, hiding regressions (`KeywordTable.tsx:42`). The fix (three-way `rankDelta` + distinct down-move rendering) and the NaN guard in `format.ts` must land before this ships. The ADR-0001 test plan is approved in principle. No security or critical issues found.", "content_type": "warning", "priority": "normal"}}

{"user_message": {"title": "Release Cut — local-seo-agency v0.2.0 (2026-05-27)", "content": "## Release v0.2.0 — Platform Migration

**Bump: MINOR** `0.1.0 → 0.2.0`  ·  Merge commit `2cd0814` "Upgrade Next.js dependencies" (author xkazm04)

### Version rationale
This is a **pre-1.0 breaking-platform migration**. Under SemVer `0.x` convention, breaking-style changes increment the **minor** version (a true MAJOR is reserved for post-1.0). The merge bumps four major framework/toolchain versions and required source/config migration — too substantial for a PATCH, but it ships **no new product features** and the app has **no public API** (`"private": true`), so it is not a feature-MINOR in the classic sense nor a real MAJOR. MINOR is the correct, consistent call.

This decision **matches the pre-drafted `CHANGELOG.md` `[0.2.0]` entry** already in the working tree — versioning stays consistent.

### What changed (from `git diff HEAD~1..HEAD`)
| Component | From | To |
|---|---|---|
| Next.js | 14.2.5 | 16.2.6 |
| React / React-DOM | 18.3.1 | 19.2.6 |
| Tailwind CSS | 3.4 | 4.3 (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) |
| TypeScript | 5.5 | 6.0 |
| ESLint | 9.13 | 10.4 |
| @google/generative-ai | 0.21 | 0.24.1 |

Config migration touched: `postcss.config.mjs` (single `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin), `src/app/globals.css` (`@import "tailwindcss"` + `@config`), `tsconfig.json` (`jsx: react-jsx`, `ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"`).

### Release notes (developer-oriented)
> **v0.2.0 — Next 16 / React 19 / Tailwind 4 / TS 6.** Breaking-platform upgrade. After pulling: re-run `npm install`. Tailwind directives are replaced by `@import "tailwindcss"`; the PostCSS pipeline now uses the single `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin (no separate `autoprefixer`/`tailwindcss` entries). Validate components against the React 19 runtime and review the Next 14→16 App Router migration notes. No product-facing feature changes.

### Artifacts produced
- ✅ `package.json` version → **0.2.0** (working tree)
- ✅ `CHANGELOG.md` `[0.2.0]` entry present and consistent (kept — not overwritten)
- ✅ Annotated tag **`v0.2.0`** created on `2cd0814` (local; **not pushed**)

### ⚠️ Action needed to publish
The version bump and changelog are **uncommitted** and the tag is **local-only**. To publish: `git add package.json CHANGELOG.md && git commit -m "chore(release): v0.2.0" && git push && git push origin v0.2.0`. See the review item below.

_Note: this release was triggered via a team-pipeline handoff (code-review → security-scan → release) on the latest merge. The security scan flagged a postcss CVE remediation that is partly addressed by this very upgrade (`postcss ^8.5.15`)._", "content_type": "success", "priority": "normal"}}
